curriculum.subject: Mathematics
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 23 chapters, and 15 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: Introduction to Polynomials, Sequences and Progressions, Exploring Algebraic Identities, Linear Equations in Two Variables
Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates, Lines and Angles, Triangles: Congruence Theorems

topics: Understanding Real Numbers and Number Sets
Properties of Natural, Whole, Integer, Rational, Irrational, and Real numbers
Representation of numbers on the number line

units.description: Introduction to Polynomials, Sequences and Progressions, Exploring Algebraic Identities, Linear Equations in Two Variables
topics: Remainder theorem, factor theorem, division algorithm (C-3.1)
Brahmagupta's quadratic formula derivation and application (C-3.3)
Sum of first n square numbers formula derivation
Proofs and Geometric Interpretations of Identities
Poetic puzzles by Bhaskara solved via quadratic formulas
Solving Linear Systems and Graphical Representation
Graphically finding the solution of a pair of linear equations (CG-4/C-5)
Finding area of a triangle with given vertices

units.description: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates, Lines and Angles, Triangles – Congruence Theorems, 4-gons (Quadrilaterals), Circles
topics: Proving theorems for triangles using Euclid's axioms (C-4.2)
Theorems on chords, subtended angles, inscribed polygons (C-4.3)
Irrationality of pi and approximations by Madhava (C-4.4)

units.description: Mensuration : Area and Perimeter, Mensuration : Surface Area and Volume
topics: Generalisation to cyclic quadrilaterals via Brahmagupta's formula
Surface Areas and Volumes of Solids
Formulas for cubes, cuboids, spheres, hemispheres (C-5.2)
Right circular cylinders or cones and their combinations
Measures of central tendency: mean, median, mode (C-6.1)
Application of probability concepts to solve problems

subtopics: Relationships between lines, angles, triangles (C-4.1)
Conjectures based on geometric properties
Proving theorems for triangles using Euclid's axioms (C-4.2)
Area-related theorems for triangles (C-7.3)
Congruence theorems for quadrilaterals (C-4.2)
Area-related theorems for parallelograms (C-7.3)

practicals.title: Lab Practical activities from prescribed books
practicals.details: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX - CBSE Publication; Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage - NCERT Publication.

activities.title: Construct the square root spiral
activities.details: Student will be able to construct the square root spiral.

activities.title: Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies, such as decomposition and step-by-step procedures to visualise algebraic identities.

activities.title: Drawing graphs of linear equations when x and y assume only certain values
activities.details: Graph a pair of linear equations.

activities.title: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates
activities.details: Specify locations and the position of one point relative to another point using coordinates.

activities.title: Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
activities.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
activities.details: Explain the construction of a square as given in the Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons
activities.details: Practice forming logical converses of statements.

activities.title: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, for example, the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, Sudarshan Chakra
activities.details: Use computational thinking to break down circle-related problems.

activities.title: Squaring a rectangle: Proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
activities.details: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape.

activities.title: Empirical probability: analysing statistical data and performing experiments
activities.details: Estimate the empirical probability of the occurrence of an event by analysing statistical data.

projects.title: Tower of Hanoi puzzle
projects.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle.

projects.title: Fractals analysis using GP
projects.details: Analyse attributes of fractals using Geometric Progressions (GP).

chapters: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.

competency_weightage.description: Competencies are integrated throughout the syllabus with emphasis on problem-solving, visualisation, mathematical modelling, communication, computational thinking, and data analytics.

internal_assessment.component: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment

internal_assessment.description: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations.

practical_assessment.details: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.

competency_weightage.description: Competencies are integrated throughout the syllabus with emphasis on problem-solving, visualisation, mathematical modelling, communication, computational thinking, and data analytics.

internal_assessment.component: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment

internal_assessment.description: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations.

practical_assessment.details: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Understands numbers and number sets
competency_groups.description: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.
competencies: C-1.1: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.

competency_groups.title: Discovers and proves algebraic identities and models real-life situations
competency_groups.description: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm; Models and solves contextualised problems using equations.
competencies: C-3.1: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm.

C-3.2: Models and solves contextualised problems using equations (for example, simultaneous linear equations in two variables or single polynomial equations), and draws conclusions about a situation being modelled.

competency_groups.title: Discovers and proves algebraic identities and models real-life situations
competency_groups.description: Learns Brahmagupta's quadratic formula (in both symbolic and poetic form) and its derivation, and uses it to solve some of the poetic puzzles of Bhaskara as well as modern-day problems.
competencies: C-3.3: Learns Brahmagupta's quadratic formula (in both symbolic and poetic form) and its derivation, and uses it to solve some of the poetic puzzles of Bhaskara as well as modern-day problems.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes
competency_groups.description: Describes relationships including congruence of two-dimensional geometric shapes to make and test conjectures and solve problems.
competencies: C-4.1: Describes relationships including congruence of two-dimensional geometric shapes (such as lines, angles, triangles) to make and test conjectures and solve problems.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes
competency_groups.description: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for triangles and quadrilaterals, and applies them to solve geometric problems.
competencies: C-4.2: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for triangles and quadrilaterals, and applies them to solve geometric problems.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes
competency_groups.description: Proves theorems about the geometry of a circle, including its chords, subtended angles, inscribed polygons, and area in terms of pi.
competencies: C-4.3: Proves theorems about the geometry of a circle, including its chords, subtended angles, inscribed polygons, and area in terms of pi.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes
competency_groups.description: Understands the irrationality of pi, the best approximations to be discovered over human history, and the first exact formula (infinite series) for pi given by Madhava.
competencies: C-4.4: Understands the irrationality of pi, the best approximations to be discovered over human history, and the first exact formula (infinite series) for pi given by Madhava.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes
competency_groups.description: Specifies locations and describes spatial relationships using coordinate geometry, for example, plotting a pair of linear equations and graphically finding the solution.
competencies: C-4.5: Specifies locations and describes spatial relationships using coordinate geometry, for example, plotting a pair of linear equations and graphically finding the solution.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes
competency_groups.description: Understands the definitions of the basic trigonometric functions, their history and motivation (including the introduction of the sin and cos functions by Aryabhata using chords), and their utility across the sciences.
competencies: C-4.6: Understands the definitions of the basic trigonometric functions, their history and motivation (including the introduction of the sin and cos functions by Aryabhata using chords), and their utility across the sciences.

competency_groups.title: Derives and uses formulae to calculate areas of plane figures, surface area, and volumes of solid objects
competency_groups.description: Visualises, represents, and calculates the area of a triangle using Heron's formula and its generalisation to cyclic quadrilaterals given by Brahmagupta's formula.
competencies: C-5.1: Visualises, represents, and calculates the area of a triangle using Heron's formula and its generalisation to cyclic quadrilaterals given by Brahmagupta's formula.

competency_groups.title: Derives and uses formulae to calculate areas of plane figures, surface area, and volumes of solid objects
competency_groups.description: Visualises and uses mathematical thinking to discover formulae to calculate surface areas and volumes of solid objects (cubes, cuboids, spheres, hemispheres, right circular cylinders or cones, and their combinations).
competencies: C-5.2: Visualises and uses mathematical thinking to discover formulae to calculate surface areas and volumes of solid objects.

competency_groups.title: Analyses and interprets data using statistical concepts and probability
competency_groups.description: Applies measures of central tendencies, such as mean, median, and mode.
competencies: C-6.1: Applies measures of central tendencies, such as mean, median, and mode.

competency_groups.title: Analyses and interprets data using statistical concepts and probability
competency_groups.description: Applies concepts from probability to solve problems on the likelihood of everyday events.
competencies: C-6.2: Applies concepts from probability to solve problems on the likelihood of everyday events.

competency_groups.title: Begins to perceive and appreciate the axiomatic and deductive structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements and carries out geometric constructions using stated assumptions, axioms, postulates, definitions, and mathematics vocabulary.
competencies: C-7.1: Proves mathematical statements and carries out geometric constructions using stated assumptions, axioms, postulates, definitions, and mathematics vocabulary.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Begins to perceive and appreciate the axiomatic and deductive structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Visualises and appreciates geometric proofs for algebraic identities and other 'proofs without words'.
competencies: C-7.2: Visualises and appreciates geometric proofs for algebraic identities and other 'proofs without words'.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Begins to perceive and appreciate the axiomatic and deductive structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, circles, area-related theorems for triangles, and parallelograms.
competencies: C-7.3: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, circles, area-related theorems for triangles, and parallelograms.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Begins to perceive and appreciate the axiomatic and deductive structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Constructs different geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles and their bisectors, triangles, and other polygons, satisfying given constraints.
competencies: C-7.4: Constructs different geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles and their bisectors, triangles, and other polygons, satisfying given constraints.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Builds skills such as visualisation, optimisation, representation, and mathematical modelling along with their application in daily life
competency_groups.description: Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions.
competencies: C-8.1: Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions.

competency_groups.title: Builds skills such as visualisation, optimisation, representation, and mathematical modelling along with their application in daily life
competency_groups.description: Uses two-dimensional representations of three-dimensional objects to visualise and solve problems.
competencies: C-8.2: Uses two-dimensional representations of three-dimensional objects to visualise and solve problems.

competency_groups.title: Builds skills such as visualisation, optimisation, representation, and mathematical modelling along with their application in daily life
competency_groups.description: Employs optimisation strategies to maximise desired quantities under given constraints.
competencies: C-8.3: Employs optimisation strategies to maximise desired quantities (such as area, volume, or other output) under given constraints.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking
competency_groups.description: Decomposes a problem into sub-problems.
competencies: C-9.1: Decomposes a problem into sub-problems.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking
competency_groups.description: Describes and analyses a sequence of instructions being followed.
competencies: C-9.2: Describes and analyses a sequence of instructions being followed.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking
competency_groups.description: Analyses similarities and differences among problems to make one solution or procedure work for multiple problems.
competencies: C-9.3: Analyses similarities and differences among problems to make one solution or procedure work for multiple problems.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking
competency_groups.description: Engages in algorithmic problem-solving to design such solutions.
competencies: C-9.4: Engages in algorithmic problem-solving to design such solutions.

competency_groups.title: Knows and appreciates important contributions of mathematicians from India and around the world
competency_groups.description: Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics.
competencies: C-10.1: Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Knows and appreciates important contributions of mathematicians from India and around the world
competency_groups.description: Recognises modern contributions to Mathematics made in both India and abroad, and understands the next frontiers.
competencies: C-10.2: Recognises modern contributions to Mathematics made in both India and abroad.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Explores connections of Mathematics with other subjects
competency_groups.description: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations in multiple subjects across Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.
competencies: C-11.1: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations in multiple subjects.

chapters: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.

competency_weightage.description: Competencies are integrated throughout the syllabus with emphasis on problem-solving, visualisation, mathematical modelling, communication, computational thinking, and data analytics.

internal_assessment.component: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment

internal_assessment.description: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations.

practical_assessment.details: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)

activities.title: Construct the square root spiral
activities.details: Student will be able to construct the square root spiral.

activities.title: Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies, such as decomposition and step-by-step procedures to visualise algebraic identities.

activities.title: Drawing graphs of linear equations when x and y assume only certain values
activities.details: Graph a pair of linear equations.

activities.title: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates
activities.details: Specify locations and the position of one point relative to another point using coordinates.

activities.title: Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
activities.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
activities.details: Explain the construction of a square as given in the Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons
activities.details: Practice forming logical converses of statements.

activities.title: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, for example, the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, Sudarshan Chakra
activities.details: Use computational thinking to break down circle-related problems.

activities.title: Squaring a rectangle: Proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
activities.details: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape.

activities.title: Empirical probability: analysing statistical data and performing experiments
activities.details: Estimate the empirical probability of the occurrence of an event by analysing statistical data.

projects.title: Tower of Hanoi puzzle
projects.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle.

projects.title: Fractals analysis using GP
projects.details: Analyse attributes of fractals using Geometric Progressions (GP).

practicals.title: Lab Practical activities from prescribed books
practicals.details: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX - CBSE Publication; Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage - NCERT Publication.
